EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER MOTION FOR ORDER TO S/A AND VACATE ORDER FOR DISMISSAL; DECLARATIN OF PLTF IN SUPPORT THEREOF; MEMORANDUM OF P/A(PROPOSED) ORDER FILED BY PLAINTIFF YAKOVLEV, ALEX
OPPOSITION TO PLTF'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE ORDER OF DISMISSAL FILED BY DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE OTHER PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF ALEX YAKOVLEV'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE ORDER FOR DISMISSAL IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW EVIDENCE OF A DEBILITATING ILLINESS, BUT EVEN IF HE HAD, THE TIME PERIOD OF THE ALLEGED ILLNESS DID NOT INTERFERE WITH HIS ABILITY TO FILE A TIMELY AMENDED COMPLAINT IN DECEMBER OR TO CONTEST THE LATER EX PARTE DISMISSAL. HIS ALLEGED FAILURE TO REALIZE THE NEED TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB
